mission.title: Install and Configure Rook Ceph Operator
mission.description: Rook Ceph Operator is a cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes that automates the deployment and management of Ceph storage. It is ideal for users looking to integrate scalable and self-managing storage solutions into their Kubernetes clusters. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management versus the flexibility and scalability of storage solutions provided.

steps.title: Add Rook Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Rook Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Rook using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Rook Helm chart repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Rook CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Rook to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.3/deploy/examples/crds.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs for Rook.

steps.title: Install Rook Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Rook operator using Helm, which manages the lifecycle of Ceph storage in your cluster.

```bash

helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --create-namespace --version v1.19.3

```

> Installs the Rook Ceph operator in the rook-ceph namespace.
commands: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --create-namespace --version v1.19.3

steps.title: Create a Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Ceph cluster using a sample configuration file, which defines the storage settings and nodes.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.3/deploy/examples/ceph/cluster.yaml

```

> This command creates a Ceph cluster using the provided configuration.

steps.title: Verify the Rook Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Rook Ceph cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod

kubectl -n rook-ceph get cephcluster

```

> Lists all pods in the rook-ceph namespace to verify their status.
> Checks the status of the Ceph cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rook Ceph Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rook Ceph operator and its resources. Be aware that this will delete all Ceph data and resources managed by Rook.

```bash

helm uninstall rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph

kubectl delete namespace rook-ceph

```

> Uninstalls the Rook Ceph operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rook Ceph Operator
upgrade.description:
This step involves backing up your data, upgrading the operator, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-ceph get cephcluster -o yaml > cephcluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3

kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod

```

> Backup the current Ceph cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the Rook Ceph operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl -n rook-ceph get cephcluster -o yaml > cephcluster-backup.yaml
helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create ceph cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration in the cluster settings.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure that the nodes meet the minimum requirements for running Ceph.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates a problem with the Ceph configuration or insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Review the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue and correct the configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Ceph cluster status shows 'HEALTH_WARN'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are issues within the Ceph cluster that need to be addressed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it <ceph-tools-pod> -- ceph health detail' to get more information on the warnings.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no available monitors'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Ceph monitor pods are not running or are misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the monitor pods and ensure they are running correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for CephFS and RBD snapshots, added new monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see all Ceph pods in the 'Running' state and the Ceph cluster status as 'HEALTH_OK' when everything is working.
codeSnippets: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --create-namespace --version v1.19.3

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources and tools installed.
